fileContext.overview: This file is the main entry point for the CodeAtlas VS Code extension, responsible for activating and deactivating the extension, handling user interactions, and managing the workflow of analyzing and indexing code.

symbols.name: activate
symbols.purpose: Activates the CodeAtlas extension, initializing secure secret storage, setting up the status bar, main panel, and activity bar, and registering commands.
symbols.behavior: Called when the extension is activated, it sets up the necessary components and registers commands for user interactions.
symbols.limitations: Must be called when the extension is activated.

symbols.name: deactivate
symbols.purpose: Deactivates the CodeAtlas extension, disposing of auto-update and server manager resources.
symbols.behavior: Called when the extension is deactivated, it disposes of resources and stops any ongoing workflows.
symbols.limitations: Must be called when the extension is deactivated.

symbols.name: setupAutoUpdate
symbols.purpose: Sets up auto-update functionality, watching for changes in the workspace and triggering incremental updates.
symbols.behavior: Called when auto-update is enabled, it sets up a file watcher and branch polling to trigger incremental updates.
symbols.limitations: Must be called when auto-update is enabled.

symbols.name: disposeAutoUpdate
symbols.purpose: Disposes of auto-update resources, stopping file watching and branch polling.
symbols.behavior: Called when auto-update is disabled, it disposes of resources and stops any ongoing workflows.
symbols.limitations: Must be called when auto-update is disabled.

symbols.name: buildWorkflowOptions
symbols.purpose: Builds workflow options based on user configuration and provider settings.
symbols.behavior: Called when building workflow options, it extracts relevant settings from the user configuration and provider settings.
symbols.limitations: Must be called when building workflow options.

symbols.name: handleWorkflowError
symbols.purpose: Handles workflow errors, displaying error messages and logging exceptions.
symbols.behavior: Called when a workflow error occurs, it displays an error message and logs the exception.
symbols.limitations: Must be called when a workflow error occurs.
